title: regex filter with case insensitive flag does not match letters with accents
description:
Hi,
Given a string literal containing letters with accents like "Wärme", the query
select ?o where {?s ?p ?o filter(regex(str(?o), 'ä', 'i'))}

does not match the literal. While
select ?o where {?s ?p ?o filter(regex(str(?o), 'ä'))}

does so, but we cannot match different cases any more. This should be a bug.
Interestingly,
ask {filter(regex('Wärme', 'ä', 'i'))}

works properly.
Many thanks for the investigation.
Best regards,
Yang
key_bug_features: regex filter with case insensitive flag fails on accented letters
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE name ILIKE '%ä%';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE name = 'Wärme' OR name = 'wärme';
